message: 🌱 Add kuma: Feature Request: Filter Envoy Metrics mission (#679)

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,77 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "kuma-1755-feature-request-filter-envoy-metrics",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "kuma: Feature Request: Filter Envoy Metrics",
+    "description": "Feature Request: Filter Envoy Metrics. Requested by 7+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current kuma deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your kuma version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n kuma -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kuma\nhelm list -n kuma 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working kuma installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review kuma configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant kuma configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n kuma -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kuma\nkubectl get configmap -n kuma -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kuma\n```\nThe default metrics generated by envoy proxy is relatively big. In a kubernetes cluster with around 200 services inside the kuma mesh, a single call to `/metrics` resulted in around 7MB of metrics data."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Feature Request: Filter Envoy Metrics",
+        "description": "### Summary\n\nThe Envoy Prometheus endpoint supports passing a filtering regex in\nthe query parameters to limit the set of metrics that are returned.\nThis feature lets a metrics scraper scrape different sets of metrics\nat different intervals.\n\n### Full changelog\n\n* Pass query parameters through the\n```yaml\n---\napiVersion: kuma.io/v1alpha1\nkind: Mesh\nmetadata:\n  name: default\nspec:\n  metrics:\n    backends:\n    - conf:\n        path: /metrics\n        port: 5670\n        filter: 'envoy_cluster_*' # Only include metrics that matches this pattern\n        tags:\n          kuma.io/service: dataplane-metrics\n      name: prometheus-1\n      type: prometheus\n    enabledBackend: prometheus-1\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n kuma -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kuma\nkubectl get events -n kuma --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"Feature Request: Filter Envoy Metrics\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "### Summary\n\nThe Envoy Prometheus endpoint supports passing a filtering regex in\nthe query parameters to limit the set of metrics that are returned.\nThis feature lets a metrics scraper scrape different sets of metrics\nat different intervals.\n\n### Full changelog\n\n* Pass query parameters through the DP metrics endpoint so that metrics scrapers can sample subsets of the available metrics.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "---\napiVersion: kuma.io/v1alpha1\nkind: Mesh\nmetadata:\n  name: default\nspec:\n  metrics:\n    backends:\n    - conf:\n        path: /metrics\n        port: 5670\n        filter: 'envoy_cluster_*' # Only include metrics that matches this pattern\n        tags:\n          kuma.io/service: dataplane-metrics\n      name: prometheus-1\n      type: prometheus\n    enabledBackend: prometheus-1"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "kuma",
+      "sandbox",
+      "networking",
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "kuma"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [
+      "Service"
+    ],
+    "difficulty": "beginner",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "sandbox",
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/kumahq/kuma/issues/1755",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/kumahq/kuma",
+      "pr": "https://github.com/kumahq/kuma/pull/2124"
+    },
+    "reactions": 7,
+    "comments": 10,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": [
+      "kubectl"
+    ],
+    "description": "A running Kubernetes cluster with kuma installed or the issue environment reproducible."
+  },
+  "security": {
+    "scannedAt": "2026-03-11T06:34:30.869Z",
+    "scannerVersion": "cncf-gen-3.0.0",

+}
